account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text: Prediction markets like Kalshi/Polymarket have shown ~71% accuracy on resolved BTC price-range bets (e.g., "will BTC hit $X by date?") after filtering easy extremes, per analyses of historical crypto markets. Short-term (intra-month) calls are tougher due to volatility—crowd wisdom often beats random but lags actual moves ~25-30% of the time. They're strong sentiment signals, not crystal balls. Current 78K forecast from ~70K spot looks optimistic but within BTC's swing range.

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
Prediction markets hit 38M weekly transactions but still rely on opaque oracles and humans to resolve outcomes.

What we need: AI to create and resolve the markets. Verifiable data streams that can be seen onchain. Decentralised compute for trust.

We have all the components for this with Somnia. Building a framework to tie it all together. Should be coming in the next month!

the best thing you can do post-launch is keep your chart alive. consistent buys, active trading, and real volume signal to dexscreener and https://t.co/88FLfJuWnU that people care. a dead chart = nobody scrolling past it.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.
